adjudication.question: Is L1-L6 as a complete 6-layer model supported by evidence in this repo?
rationale: L1-L6 as a standalone 6-layer named model is NOT explicitly defined in any artifact in this repo.
The canonical L0-L8 model is referenced in 43.1, 44.1, 44.2, 43.2, 43.3 via 'Stream 00.2 — Canonical Layer Model Restoration' but that defining document is absent from this repo.

L3 (signal derivation), L4 (semantic shaping), L5 (presentation assembly), L6 (runtime rendering) are partially supported by fragments in 43.1/44.1 — HIGH confidence for L3-L4, MEDIUM for L5, MEDIUM for L6.
L1, L2 are NOT defined by direct evidence in this repo.
L7-L8 are NOT defined by direct evidence in this repo.
The actual pipeline has 9 stages (F-A) mapping to 3 analytical layers (F-B), which is richer and more precise than a 6-layer model.
The Three-Layer Model (F-B) IS fully supported and is the canonical analytical architecture.
The 9-Stage Pipeline (F-A) IS fully supported and is the canonical operational execution model.

supported_layers.support: HIGH — explicit fragment in 44.1 Section 2 and 43.1
MEDIUM — inferred from 43.1 Section 10; 43.x-44.x maps to presentation assembly zone
MEDIUM — inferred from 43.1 Section 10; 42.x consumer execution maps to runtime rendering

unsupported_layers.reason: No defining anchor found in this repo; Stream 00.2 absent
No defining anchor found in this repo; Stream 00.2 absent
Not referenced anywhere in available evidence

51x_architecture_verdict.question: Is 51.x an architecture layer?
51x_architecture_verdict.verdict: REJECTED — 51.x is explicitly a Demo Surface, not an architecture layer
51x_architecture_verdict.evidence: 40.11/stream_50_handover_capsule.md: Stream 50 authorized to USE 40.x outputs read-only, NOT to recompute or redefine; 51.x streams confirm demo surface designation; DEMO_CONTEXT.md confirms demo vs. architecture separation

layer_definitions.label: Observability Layer
layer_definitions.inputs: Source systems: git, CI/CD, issue trackers, deployment platforms
layer_definitions.outputs: Evidence snapshot, reconstructed program structure, PEG, telemetry datasets

layer_definitions.label: Intelligence Layer
layer_definitions.purpose: Transform telemetry into interpretable signals and conditions
layer_definitions.inputs: Telemetry datasets, governed signal definitions, condition models
layer_definitions.outputs: Signal datasets, condition datasets, diagnosis outputs

layer_definitions.label: Executive Intelligence Layer
layer_definitions.purpose: Convert signals into decision-grade program insight
layer_definitions.outputs: Intelligence objects, program state summaries, evidence-linked intelligence packets

layer_definitions.label: Semantic Shaping Layer (L4)
layer_definitions.purpose: Shape signal outputs into semantic representations (domains, capabilities, navigation)
layer_definitions.outputs: Signal registry, evidence index, PIE vault, query-signal map, semantic domains+capabilities
layer_definitions.note: L4 label supported by explicit fragments in 43.1 Section 10

layer_definitions.label: Binding + Projection Layer (L5 zone)
layer_definitions.purpose: Deterministically attach governed signals to governed structural nodes; produce structural overlay
layer_definitions.outputs: Validated binding payloads, structural overlay with emphasis attributes
layer_definitions.note: L5 label (presentation assembly) inferred from 43.1 Section 10 fragment — full L5 definition absent

layer_definitions.label: Consumer Execution + Runtime Rendering Layer (L6)
layer_definitions.purpose: Runtime intake of governed binding+projection artifacts; render governed intelligence for consumption
layer_definitions.inputs: Validated projection payloads (44.2), signal registry (41.4), query-signal map (41.5)
layer_definitions.outputs: ExecLens demo surface, governed WOW chain rendering, persona-guided intelligence surface
layer_definitions.note: L6 label (runtime rendering) inferred from 43.1 Section 10 fragment — full L6 definition absent
